mission.title: Install and Configure MetalLB (Bare-metal Load Balancer)
mission.description: MetalLB is a load-balancer implementation for bare metal Kubernetes clusters, utilizing standard routing protocols. It is ideal for environments where cloud-native load balancing is not available, providing a way to expose services externally. Key trade-offs include the need for manual configuration of IP address pools and reliance on the underlying network infrastructure.

steps.title: Add MetalLB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official MetalLB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add metallb https://metallb.github.io/metallb

```

> This command sets up the repository for installing MetalLB.

steps.title: Install MetalLB Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for MetalLB to function correctly, as they define the custom resources used by MetalLB.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/namespace.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metallb/metallb/v0.15.3/manifests/metallb.yaml

```

> Creates the MetalLB namespace.
> Deploys the MetalLB components.

steps.title: Install MetalLB using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs MetalLB into the Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the version and namespace.

```bash

helm install metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

```

> Installs MetalLB in the metallb-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a MetalLB configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a ConfigMap to define the IP address pool that MetalLB will use for load balancing.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

```

> Replace the IP range with your own available IPs.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

steps.title: Verify MetalLB installation
steps.description:
This step checks if MetalLB is running correctly and if the services are exposed properly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

kubectl get svc -n metallb-system

```

> Ensure all MetalLB pods are in the Running state.
> Check if the MetalLB service is available.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MetalLB
uninstall.description:
This step removes MetalLB from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and configurations, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall metallb --namespace metallb-system

kubectl delete namespace metallb-system

```

> Removes the MetalLB release.
> Deletes the MetalLB namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MetalLB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step involves draining the nodes if necessary, upgrading MetalLB, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

helm upgrade metallb metallb/metallb --namespace metallb-system --version 0.15.3

kubectl get pods -n metallb-system

```

> Backup the current MetalLB configuration.
> Upgrades MetalLB to the specified version.
> Verify that all MetalLB pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get configmap config -n metallb-system -o yaml > metallb-config-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'service is not exposed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if MetalLB is not configured correctly or the IP address pool is exhausted.

**Fix:**
Check the MetalLB configuration and ensure the IP address range is valid and not in use.

troubleshooting.title: MetalLB pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there is a misconfiguration in the MetalLB settings.

**Fix:**
Inspect the logs of the MetalLB pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metallb-system' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Load balancer service does not get an external IP
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may indicate that MetalLB is not able to allocate an IP from the defined pool.

**Fix:**
Verify that the IP address pool is correctly defined and that the addresses are not already in use.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no available IP addresses'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the configured IP address range is fully allocated.

**Fix:**
Expand the IP address range in the MetalLB configuration or free up some addresses.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for BGP mode, improved layer 2 mode stability, and added metrics endpoint.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.14, ensure to review the new BGP configuration options.

resolution.summary: You should see the MetalLB pods running in the 'Running' state and services with external IPs assigned when you run 'kubectl get svc'.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  namespace: metallb-system
  name: config
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.168.1.200-192.168.1.250
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster is set up and accessible before installing MetalLB.
